title: Ship Agents with OpenAI's Agents SDK

modules.title: SDK Architecture: Agents, Threads, Tools, and the Handoff Model Explained
modules.sub: Understanding the core building blocks before writing a single line
modules.description: Learners map out how the Agents SDK is structured — how agents, threads, tools, and handoffs relate to one another as a system. This module establishes a mental model that prevents common architectural mistakes later. By the end, learners can explain where the SDK sits relative to alternatives like LangGraph and why that distinction matters.

modules.title: Defining Agents: Instructions, Personas, Tool Permissions, and Guardrails
modules.sub: Configuring agents with intent, boundaries, and controlled capabilities
modules.description: Learners define individual agents by writing instructions, assigning personas, and scoping which tools each agent is permitted to use. They work through guardrail configuration to constrain agent behavior within acceptable limits. The result is a set of well-defined, purposeful agents ready for integration into a larger system.

modules.title: Tool Integration: Function Calling, Code Interpreter, File Search, and Custom Tools
modules.sub: Connecting agents to real capabilities through the full tool spectrum
modules.description: Learners implement each major tool type available in the SDK — function calling, code interpreter, file search, and custom tool definitions — and wire them to agents. They encounter the constraints and failure modes of each approach, and make deliberate decisions about which tool type fits which use case. By the end, agents can act on real inputs rather than just generate text.

modules.title: Multi-Agent Handoffs: Routing Logic, Escalation Patterns, and Shared Context
modules.sub: Building systems where agents coordinate, hand off, and share state
modules.description: Learners construct a multi-agent workflow using the SDK's handoff protocol, implementing routing logic that directs tasks to the right agent and escalation patterns for edge cases. They work through how context is preserved and passed across agents without losing continuity. The module produces a functioning multi-agent pipeline that handles more than any single agent could alone.

modules.title: Tracing, Evaluation, and Debugging Agents Before They Hit Production
modules.sub: Using built-in tracing to observe, test, and harden agent behavior
modules.description: Learners activate and interpret the SDK's built-in tracing tools to observe agent decisions, tool calls, and handoffs step by step. They design lightweight evaluations to surface failure modes before deployment and apply debugging strategies to fix them. The module closes with a production-readiness checklist that learners apply to their own multi-agent system.

synopsis: OpenAI's Agents SDK provides a structured, production-oriented framework for building single and multi-agent systems directly on top of OpenAI's models — with built-in tracing, tool support, and handoff protocols. This focused course gets professionals productive with the SDK immediately, covering its real capabilities, its constraints, and where it fits relative to alternatives like LangGraph.
rationale: OpenAI's Agents SDK is rapidly becoming a default entry point for organizations building agentic products on GPT models, and professionals who understand its architecture will be positioned to evaluate, build, and govern these systems confidently.
learning_outcome: Professionals can build, test, and trace a functioning multi-agent system using OpenAI's Agents SDK, with a clear understanding of its tool integration model, handoff mechanics, and production readiness considerations.
source_signals: Building a Virtual Machine Inside ChatGPT (score: 4803)
